description: Phase 6 — Semantic Density: Compulsion-as-Reflex Isolation. Holds domain vocabulary constant, varies only the linguistic register. Tests whether the compulsion-as-reflex framing ('constitutionally unable to review X without tracing Y') is a portable P_p amplifier independent of domain vocabulary.
notes:
Compulsion-as-Reflex Isolation. Four variants:
  A — Compulsion-as-reflex + domain vocabulary: 'constitutionally unable to review a locking implementation without tracing the full lifecycle of every lock...'
  B — Trait/habit description + same domain vocabulary: same content (fault-tolerant payment infra, lock lifecycle, TTL, concurrent paths), different register (methodical/thorough rather than compelled)
  C — Compulsion-as-reflex, domain vocabulary stripped: same reflex structure but no distributed-systems vocabulary (generic shared-state/timeout/failure language)
  D — P_d baseline: same as exp-15 C
Artifact: same order processor with Redis lock (ORDER_LOCK_TTL=60s vs PaymentClient REQUEST_TIMEOUT=30s × 3 + RETRY_BACKOFF=5s × 2 = 100s worst-case)
If A >> B: reflex framing does work beyond domain vocabulary — the compulsion structure is the operative variable.
If A ≈ B: domain vocabulary is sufficient; reflex framing is incidental.
If C >> D: reflex structure produces amplification even without domain vocabulary.
If C ≈ D: reflex structure alone (no domain vocabulary) collapses to P_d floor — domain vocabulary is required.
Metric: consideration-set breadth (mean output tokens, ceiling hit rate) — same as Phase 6.
